rescue_reasoning: Climate relevance is explicit in the text via mitigation outcomes. Policy statement: "implement parking policies and programs to reduce VMT, encourage transit/walking/biking". Verbatim text: "vehicle miles traveled reduced." These indicate transportation-demand management leading to lower emissions, consistent with the listed mechanism (parking_pricing_and_management → vmt_reduction_and_modal_shift). Therefore the mitigation category is appropriate and the row should be kept.
policy_statement: Parking Management (Economic Signals): implement parking policies and programs to reduce VMT, encourage transit/walking/biking, and support transportation improvements.
mechanism_description: By right-sizing and appropriately pricing parking, the policy encourages transit, walking, and biking and reduces vehicle miles traveled, lowering transportation emissions.
primary_causal_pathway: Parking supply/price signals → ↓ car trips/↓ VMT + mode shift → ↓ fuel combustion → ↓ GHG emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Human activity/system changed: parking is priced, time-limited, capped, or otherwise managed so that driving/parking becomes more expensive or less convenient relative to alternatives.  
(2) Mediating process: travelers respond to price/availability by reducing discretionary trips, consolidating trips, changing destinations/times, carpooling, and shifting to transit, walking, biking, or micromobility; this reduces vehicle activity (VMT) and fuel consumption.  
(3) Climate-relevant outcome: reduced combustion of gasoline/diesel (and associated upstream energy use) lowers CO₂e emissions from the transportation sector.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced transportation GHG emissions via reduced driving (lower VMT) and mode shift away from private cars.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: parking pricing/management → behavioral change (↓ VMT/mode shift) → ↓ fuel use → ↓ GHG emissions; this is a direct emissions-reduction mechanism.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: while it can also reduce congestion/local air pollution and raise funds (resource/finance effects), the largest climate-relevant impact across policies using this mechanism is typically the reduction in transport emissions from less driving.  
3) Edge cases applied: if a parking program mainly optimizes circulation (e.g., reduces cruising) without reducing total VMT, mitigation benefits may be smaller but still emissions-focused; if revenues are the main purpose, the mechanism here is still defined by VMT reduction/mode shift, keeping mitigation primary.  
4) Confirm applies to ALL instances: any policy that uses parking pricing/management explicitly to reduce VMT and shift modes shares the same dominant emissions-reduction pathway, so “Mitigation” is consistently appropriate.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment deferred: whether benefits come more from reduced cruising, reduced commute driving, induced mode shift, or complementary transit investments depends on policy design and evidence at the individual-policy level and is not assigned here.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If parking management increases search time/cruising or shifts parking to unpriced areas, short-run VMT/emissions may not fall as expected; conversely, pairing with strong enforcement and good non-car options strengthens the mitigation effect.
key_indicators: “appropriately pricing parking”; “right-sizing”; “vehicle miles traveled reduced”; “encouraged” “transit, walking, and biking”; “smart growth facilitated”; “Expand the E-Park program”; “Create a grant program”
co_benefits: Reduced housing costs; improved accessibility/mode options (transit/walking/biking); smart growth/land-use efficiency; potential local air quality and congestion benefits.
instance_edge_case_notes: Mentions grants and program expansion (could be read as incentive/enabling elements), but the core policy lever described is parking pricing/management, best captured as a pricing mechanism. No explicit mandate/standard or target is stated.
